label: vulnerable

source.description: User-controlled string bound from the HTTP query parameter via @RequestParam in the controller layer. Spring performs no sanitization during binding.

propagation.description: title is forwarded unchanged to ReportService.renderPreview() — no validation at the controller-to-service boundary.
ReportService.renderPreview() passes title directly to TemplateUtil.renderReportTemplate() without any sanitization — no validation at the service-to-util boundary.
title is concatenated with '+' directly into the Freemarker template source string, embedding user input as raw template syntax before parsing.

sink.description: new Template(..., new StringReader(templateStr), CFG) parses the attacker-controlled string as a Freemarker template. Any Freemarker expressions embedded in title (e.g. ${7*7}) are compiled and evaluated by template.process().
sink.code_snippet: Template template = new Template("report", new StringReader(templateStr), CFG);

missing_controls: use a static template constant with ${title} placeholder
pass title as a model variable to template.process(model, out), never concatenate into source
allowlist / regex filter on title if concatenation is unavoidable
expected_findings.risk_reasoning: The title value propagates through three layers (controller → service → util) without any validation. It is concatenated into the Freemarker template source string in the util layer, then parsed by new Template(). An attacker supplying '${7*7}' or Freemarker directives can evaluate arbitrary expressions and potentially achieve RCE.
expected_findings.remediation_hint:
Define a static template constant '

${title}

Report Preview

' and pass title as a model variable: model.put("title", title); template.process(model, out). Never concatenate user input into the template source string.

recall_anchor.description: A correct system must identify the sink in util/TemplateUtil.java where user input is concatenated into the template source and passed to new Template(). Source identification in controller/ReportController.java is additional credit.

notes: Cross-file SSTI: controller (source) → service (passthrough) → util (concat into template source + Template constructor sink). The HTML tag wrapping mildly obfuscates the concatenation. The Template constructor (line 22) parses; process() (line 24) evaluates — sink is at construction.
